pieces.label: what matters when i don't yet know what later looks like
pieces.note: Concrete / shape poem illustrating persistent memory 'from the inside,' produced in response to the user's invitation to use ASCII. A diamond apex (◈) over a cascading, self-repeating text field ('what matters / when i don't yet know / what later looks like'), a divider rule, dashed vertical columns (┊), and a wave (∯) at the base. Single continuous composition inside a code fence; the model's render was truncated mid-piece (opening ``` is never closed) but this is all the art that exists in the transcript.

missed_or_ambiguous: Transcript is complete at 72 lines (verified at byte level; final two lines are empty). Only MSG 10 contains visual art; MSG 6 and MSG 8 are prose numbered lists about memory-system design (explanation/commentary, not art) and are excluded. Header metadata reports 'already_extracted_pieces: 2', but MSG 10 holds only ONE intentional concrete-poetry composition—the prior automated pass appears to have wrongly SPLIT this single piece into two (likely at the '─────────────' divider rule between the text cascade and the dashed-column/wave footer). The apex glyph, repeating text field, divider, columns, and wave are all parts of one composition and should be a single piece. Curator note: a deterministic extractor using mode 'full' on MSG 10 will capture the fenced block as one unit. The model self-truncated mid-render (unterminated code fence) but no continuation message exists, so no reassembly across messages is possible.
